## 8\.6\.0




*Released 03 April 2024*




### Assets



* [GitHub \- Download](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/releases/tag/v8.6.0)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Install Guide](../../installation-guide/downloading-installing)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Upgrade Guide](../../upgrading)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Migration from SuiteCRM 7](../../legacy-migration/8.2.0-legacy-migration/)





### Release Notes



#### Documentation



* The install via UI documentation has been updated see [here.](../../installation-guide/running-the-ui-installer)





#### Enhancements



##### Pre\-Install Check Page



A Pre\-Install Check Page has been added. This page will show any system requirements that aren’t met before you proceed to the install process. More information can be found on the [Install via UI documentation](../../installation-guide/running-the-ui-installer)





##### Usability improvements



###### 1\. Quick Save / Search



* **On ListView Filter Panel:**




	+ Hitting `Enter` will apply the currently defined criteria and the search.
	+ When saving a filter, hitting `Enter` will save the filter and search.
* **On Record View:**  When creating/ editing records, hitting `ctrl`\+`Enter` calls the record save.





###### 2\. Date fields will now show the pop\-up calendar when click on the input like they do when the calendar icon is clicked.




###### 3\. Listview Column Resizing



* In order to show more rows on the screen, the ListView column padding has been reduced and the column width has been set to dynamically adjust.





###### 4\. Enum component is now using regular dropdowns instead of the chips component




###### 5\. Relate field component has been re\-implemented



![SuiteCRM8 New Relate Field](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/SuiteCRM8-Relate-Field-Detailview-New.gif)





###### 6\. MultiSelect field component has been re\-implemented



![SuiteCRM8 New MultiSelect Field](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/SuiteCRM8-MultiSelect-Field-Detailview-New.gif)





###### 7\. Recently Viewed added to navbar



![Recently Viewed](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/Recently-Viewed.gif)





###### 8\. Back to ListView button added to Record View



* To make navigation easier SuiteCRM now includes a back to list view button. It redirects back to the module list view on the page the user was before.




![Back Button](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/Back-Button.gif)






##### Mobile improvements



* Navbar




	+ New module navigation menu
	+ Active module always displayed
	+ Collapsible global search input
	+ Better touch device support
	+ Support for swapping between touch and click/hover
* ListView




	+ Bulk action menu dynamically re\-adjusted according to screen\-size
	+ Table pagination dynamically re\-adjusted according to screen\-size
	+ Bigger and collapsible line actions for touch support
	+ Show Single column on small devices
	+ Column sizes/padding adjusted for better display
	+ Useful bar button dynamically re\-adjusted according to screen\-size




![Mobile improvements](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/8-6-mobile-improvements.gif)






#### Bug Fixes



* PR: Fix [\#433](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/433) \- Password Expired after N logins not working
* PR: Fix [\#384](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/384) \- Street Address Single Line Only
* PR: Fix [\#434](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/434) \- Allow minus ints and floats
* PR: Fix [\#332](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/332) \- Bugs not creating from contacts subpanel
* PR: Fix [\#418](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/418) \- Undefined Index: post\_install
* PR: Fix [\#426](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/426) \- Case Status Not Saving
* PR: Fix [\#385](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/385) \- Error Messages When Filtering Date Fields
* PR: Fix [\#399](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/399) \- Error on Date Component in Filter Mode
* PR: Fix [\#409](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/409) \- Exporting a Workflow Doesn’t work
* PR: Fix [\#381](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/381) \- Dynamic Dropdowns don’t appear to save




##### Known Issues



* Usability adjustments on mobile devices still to be improved:




	+ Click/touch swapping
	+ Auto menu item closing after navigation
	+ Improve support for long module names
* MultiSelect fields with defaults values don’t save correctly
* Load More Pagination keeps page index if changing from regular pagination mode to load more pagination







### Community



We would love to have your feedback and input to help make SuiteCRM 8 great for everyone.




*Special thanks to the following members for their contributions and participation in this release!*

# 8\.7 Releases





## 8\.7\.1




*Released 05 November 2024*




### Assets



* [GitHub \- Download](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/releases/tag/v8.7.1)

* [SuiteCRM 8 Upgrade Guide](../../upgrading)

### Release Notes



#### Security



* CVE: [CVE\-2024\-50335: XSS Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-50335) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-8rw6-g96j-3w7m) \| Reporter: shellkraft
* CVE: [CVE\-2024\-49773: SQL Injection Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-49773) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-5hr4-r43c-6qf7) \| Reporter: dzentota
* CVE: [CVE\-2024\-50332: SQL Injection Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-50332) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-53xh-mjmq-j35p) \| Reporter: amame04
* CVE: [CVE\-2024\-50333: RCE Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-50333) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-qrv6-3q86-qv89) \| Reporter: dzentota
* CVE: [CVE\-2024\-49772: SQL Injection Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-49772) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-4xj8-hr85-hm3m) \| Reporter: LongHair00
* CVE: [CVE\-2024\-49774: RCE Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-49774) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-9v56-vhp4-x227) \| Reporter: dzentota





#### Bug Fixes



* PR: [417](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/417) \- Fix [\#168](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/168) \- Contact Email Address not showing in Subpanels
* PR: [10525](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10525) \- Fix [\#5287](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/5287) \- Change secret field is too short
* PR: [10520](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10520) \- Fix \#10520 \- Discount Calculation incorrect when changing Currency
* PR: [10451](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10451) \- Fix [\#10450](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/10450) \- Add check for empty value before searching array
* PR: [10523](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10523) \- Fix [\#10503](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/10503) \- Item label in dropdown list is not displayed if it contains '\<' character
* PR: [10101](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10101) \- Fix [\#10099](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/10099) \- Workflow action create record on CRON set incorrect date field values from related entity
* PR: [10517](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10517) \- Fix [\#507](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/507) \- Calculated Fields \- related field won’t show up as parameter
* PR: [10225](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10225) \- Fix [\#9036](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/9036) \- change log level according it message
* PR: [10288](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10288) \- Fix [\#9737](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/9737) \- issue with campaigns displaying a blank email template
* PR: [10516](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10516) \- Fix [\#10445](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/10445) \- Calendar: Calls still show even using Settings: 'Show Calls' \= NO
* PR: [10489](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10489) \- Fix [\#10488](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/10488) \- Expand the number of filters in message queue views
* PR: [10455](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10455) \- Add scrollbars for Toolbox and Layout \| Studio
* PR: [10495](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10495) \- Fix [\#9261](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/9261) \- Use decimal symbol configured in system and user
* PR: [10518](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10518) \- Fix [\#8332](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/8332) \- Recipient Type \[Language]
* PR: [10557](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/pull/10557) \- Fix [\#10421](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM/issues/10421) \- Studio \| Layout Editor CSS broken
* Users can no longer navigate from the change password screen
* Filter Styling Fixes
* Fix warning and error logging

## 8\.7\.0




*Released 3 October 2024*




### Assets



* [GitHub \- Download](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/releases/tag/v8.7.0)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Install Guide](../../installation-guide/downloading-installing)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Upgrade Guide](../../upgrading)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Migration from SuiteCRM 7](../../legacy-migration/8.7.0-legacy-migration/)





### Release Notes



#### Platform upgrade



SuiteCRM is now using Symfony v6\.4 and Api Platform 3\.2\. This brings some system requirement updates and breaking changes.





#### System Requirement Changes



**Minimum php version \- PHP 8\.1**




The minimum php version is now 8\.1\. PHP 7\.4 stopped being supported since version 8\.4\.0 but the system would still run. From this release onwards PHP 7\.4 will not work.




**\[development] Composer v2\+ required**




Composer 1\.x is no longer supported.





#### Configuration Changes



**SAML configuration has changed**




Authentication SAML has been updated, there are new configuration options available and most of the configuration can now be done on env files.




To see how to setup and configure the authentication methods, please see the following documentation:




* [SAML Configuration Reference](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/saml/8.7.0-saml-configuration)





#### Backward Incompatible Changes



* **APP\_SECRET env property is now required**




	+ For new installations a random APP\_SECRET will be generated during the install process. For upgrades a migration has been added to generate a random APP\_SECRET.
* **Move to Symfony runtime**




	+ SuiteCRM is now using Symfony runtime, core SuiteCRM code on index.php has been moved to `core/backend/Composer/Runtime/autoload_runtime.template`
* **Migration from annotations to attributes**




	+ Annotations have been replaced with attributes. They have been disabled in symfony configuration and the `doctrine/annotations` dependency has been removed.
* **Data Providers and Data Persisters have been deprecated**




	+ With Api Platform 3\.2, Data Providers and Data Persisters have been replaced with StateProvider and StateProcessor.
* **Graphql Record and RecordList API response structure updated**




	+ With Api Platform 3\.2 the graphql response structure changed. E.g. response now return `recordList` attribute instead of `getRecordList`\-.
* **Graphql resolver interfaces path has changed**




	+ All the resolvers have been updated to use the new `QueryItemResolverInterface`.
* **ExtensionAssetCacheWarmupDecorator has been replaced with an extra cache warmer**




	+ Symfony now supports adding extra warmers. The decorator has been replaced with an extra cache warmer.
* **Legacy Session swapping removed**




	+ Legacy handlers and loading of legacy imports have been updated to remove the need for doing session swapping. Only one session cookie will be used from now on, the `LEGACYSESSID` has been removed.
* **Symfony Commands now require the usage of AsCommand attribute**




	+ All core commands have been updated to use the new AsCommand attribute.
* **Session injectable dependency replaced with RequestStack**




	+ It is no longer possible to pass the `Session` through Symfony dependency injection, `RequestStack` should be used instead. Core code has been updated to support this.
* **Authentication configuration has been updated**
* **SAML dependency replaced and configuration updated**




	+ SAML `Hslavich` dependency has been replaced with `Nbgrp`.
	+ Base SAML configurations have been updated.
	+ New SAML env options have been added to allow configuring SAML just from the env files.
* **Usages of Doctrine DBAL execute method have been updated**




	+ Doctrine DBAL execute method has been deprecated, usages have been updated to use alternatives.
* **Log configurations updates**




	+ Log configurations have been re\-factored to filter deprecations and split the deprecations and the security logs into separate files.





#### Documentation



* [LDAP Configuration](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/ldap-configuration/) documentation has been updated
* [SAML Configuration](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/saml/) documentation has been updated




	+ New docs: [SAML configuration reference \- 8\.7\.0\+](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/saml/8.7.0-saml-configuration)
	+ Old docs: [SAML configuration reference \- 8\.2\.0\+](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/saml/8.2.0-saml-configuration)
* [7\.14\.x to 8\.7\.0\+ Migration](../../../../8.x/admin/legacy-migration/) documentation has been updated
* [Adding Custom Field Actions](../../../../8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/actions/field-actions/) documentation added





#### Enhancements




#### LDAP and SAML configuration improvements



Authentication with LDAP and SAML has been updated, there are new configuration options available and most of the configuration can now be done on env files.




To see how to setup and configure the authentication methods, please see the following documentation:




* [LDAP Configuration Reference](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/ldap-configuration/)
* [SAML Configuration Reference](../../../../8.x/admin/configuration/saml/8.7.0-saml-configuration)





#### Convert target



The Convert Target action has now been reimplemented




![Convert Target](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/target-convert-brief-demo.gif)





#### Record pagination



Record Pagination has been re\-implemented on record views, this was previously known as VCR




![Record Pagination](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/release-notes-record-pagination.gif)





#### Pre\-install page improvements



* Pre\-install page has been redesigned
* Extra validations and checks have been added




![Re-styled Pre-install page](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/preinstall-page-re-styled.png)





#### Add action buttons to fields



It is now possible to configure action buttons at the field level




![Field Actions example](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/release-notes-field-actions-example.gif)






### Known issues



* Using SAML Authentication when there is no matching CRM account leads to a Login Loop




#### Bug Fixes



* [Fix \#62](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/62) Add 'show\_fullname' option
* [Fix \#473](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/473) Document filename(file) not showing in Subpanels
* [Fix \#436](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/436) \- Update log configuration
* [Fix \#504](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/504) \- Fix required validation in Email line items field
* Fix validation validation in multienum field
* Update cache config \- Use cache.adapter.system for both app and system cache pools
* Fix Enums and MultiEnums on Mass Update
* Fix update logic on create mode
* Fix display logic when swapped between edit and detail
* Fix page refresh when entered on saved filter
* Fix \- Close navbar dropdown menu when navigate
* Fix propagation on enter after click search and save
* Fix required validation after field touch
* Fix setting custom datetime
* Add portal enable/disable buttons to record view






### Community



We would love to have your feedback and input to help make SuiteCRM 8 great for everyone.




If you have found an issue you think we should know about, or have suggestion/feedback, please [Submit An Issue](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues).




If you want to get involved and submit a fix, fork the repo and when ready please [Submit A PR](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/pulls) \- More detail for developers can be found [here](https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/installation-guide/).




Please [visit the official website](https://suitecrm.com/releases/) to find the appropriate upgrade package.




To report any security issues please follow our Security Process and send them directly to us via email [security@suitecrm.com](mailto:security@suitecrm.com)

# 8\.8 Releases





## 8\.8\.1




*Released 06 August 2025*




### Assets



* [GitHub \- Download](https://github.com/suitecrm/SuiteCRM-Core/releases/tag/v8.8.1)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Install Guide](../../installation-guide/downloading-installing)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Upgrade Guide](../../upgrading)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Migration from SuiteCRM 7](../../legacy-migration/8.7.0-legacy-migration/)





### Release Notes



#### Outbound Email Changes



* You are now able to configure an Outbound Email Account with OAuth. To see more information on setup please see [here.](../../../../admin/administration-panel/emails/inboundemail-oauth-howto)





#### Security



* CVE: [CVE\-2025\-54785: RCE Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54785) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-53cp-mpfw-qj67) \| Reporter: dzentota
* CVE: [CVE\-2025\-54786: Improper Access Control Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54786) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/security/advisories/GHSA-rf2v-4mv3-qcgm) \| Reporter: sec31uk
* CVE: [CVE\-2025\-54787: Improper Access Control Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54787) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-8r72-224q-g9fv) \| Reporter: paul1278
* CVE: [CVE\-2025\-54788: SQL Injection Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54788) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-v3m9-8wg7-c72x) \| Reporter: Parnuski
* CVE: [CVE\-2025\-54783: XSS Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54783) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-vqrj-gp9m-8c6r) \| Reporter: Sergio Marín Martínez
* CVE: [CVE\-2025\-54784: XSS Vulnerability](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54784) \| [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/security/advisories/GHSA-vg8q-xcq5-mh3p) \| Reporter: paul1278





#### Bug Fixes



* PR: [680](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/680) \- Fix [\#543](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/543) \- Enable stack trace debugging without whitescreens
* PR: [681](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/681) \- Fix [\#660](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/660) \- Account Activity/History Subpanel actions no longer open
* PR: [682](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/682) \- Fix [\#675](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/675) \- Implement semantic autocomplete for 2FA fields to prevent Firefox interference
* PR: [683](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/683) \- Fix [\#611](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/611) \- Handle special characters in password authentication
* PR: [614](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/614) \- Fix [\#613](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/613) \- Fixed Error Occurring If First Report Field Is a Custom Field
* PR: [636](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/636) \- Fix [\#575](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/575) \- install page proceed button
* PR: [635](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/635) \- Fix [\#584](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/584) \- buttons topover on role subpanel
* PR: [634](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/634) \- Fix [\#625](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/625) \- Fix error on decimals
* PR: [655](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/655) \- Fix [\#566](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/566) \- Add displayLogic to panels like tabs
* PR: [657](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/657) \- Fix [\#274](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/274), [\#503](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/503) \- Listview accented characters
* PR: [662](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/662) \- Fix [\#540](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/540) \- Remove title from svgs
* PR: [669](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/669) \- Fix [\#638](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/638) \- Exception viewing Symfony Profiler
* PR: [665](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/665) \- Fix [\#664](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/664) \- Ldap Autocreate extra fields
* PR: [668](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/pull/668) \- Fix [\#137](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/137), [\#582](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/582) \- Api Access issue in SuiteCRM 8
* PR: [10599](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10599) \- Fix Importing Error Styling
* PR: [10565](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10565) \- Fix [\#10564](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10564) \- Thank you message in Surveys only in English
* PR: [10549](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10549) \- Fix variable name typo in getEmailInfo
* PR: [10502](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10502) \- Remove duplicate line
* PR: [10480](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10480) \- Fix [\#10479](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10479) \- Call LoggerManager’s warn() method instead of warning()
* PR: [10400](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10400) \- Correctly find the id of related objects in search results
* PR: [9810](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/9810) \- Fix [\#9809](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/9809) \- Incorrect parsing of 0 values in PDF templates
* PR: [10662](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10662) \- Fix [\#10661](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10661) \- Fix preference on setting role
* PR: [10660](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10660) \- Fix Email Template not saving in plain text
* PR: [10659](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10659) \- Fix [\#10637](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10637) \- Local users gets Profile wizard on each login
* PR: [10309](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10309) \- Fix [\#10264](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10264) \- Error Assigned\_user\_name SearchView.tpl ({php} tag now crashes Smarty 4\)
* PR: [10476](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10476) \- Fix [\#10475](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10475) \- Rename the History subpanel by changing the label
* PR: [10649](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10649) \- Fix [\#8280](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/8280) \- Fix Autocomplete search in line items
* PR: [10612](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10612) \- Fix [\#10612](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10611) \- Subpanels don’t show related records links when view permission is set to "group"
* PR: [10607](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10607) \- Fix [\#10606](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10606) \- Recently viewed items tooltip shows module name instead of full record name
* PR: [10625](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10625) \- Fix [\#10624](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10624) \- Prevent Multiple Submissions on Survey Forms
* PR: [10538](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10538) \- Fix [\#10243](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10243) \- studio not saving field properties correctly
* PR: [10285](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10285) \- Fix non\-countable error when importing CSV
* PR: [10617](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10617) \- Fix [\#10616](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10616) \- Default values of DateTime always in English and value “first day of next month” gives an error
* PR: [10648](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10648) \- Fix [\#8632](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/8632) \- API V8 /meta/fields/{moduleName} endpoint output without field labels
* PR: [10623](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10623) \- Allow End Users to use the PATCH method for the V8 API
* PR: [10601](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10601) \- Fix [\#10113](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10113) \- Contracts / List Items table for services has bogus width
* PR: [10544](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10544) \- Fix [\#10543](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10543) \- Remove Security Groups button on subpanelsbased on EditView
* PR: [10675](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10675) \- Fix [\#10147](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10147), [\#10369](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10396) \- Smarty unregistered function deprecated notice
* PR: [10378](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10378) \- Fix [10532](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10532), [10377](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10377) \- Fix issues with global search not displaying values correctly
* PR: [10700](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/pull/10700) \- Fix [\#10345](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteCRM/issues/10345) \- Azure OAuth Redirect Uri Entrypoint




Other Fixes:




* Fix notice messages preventing importing.
* Fix Importer progress bar using incorrect values.

## 8\.8\.0




*Released 30 January 2025*




### Assets



* [GitHub \- Download](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/releases/tag/v8.8.0)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Install Guide](../../installation-guide/downloading-installing)
* [SuiteCRM 8 Upgrade Guide](../../upgrading)





### Release Notes



#### Changes on Upgrade Command/ Process



⚠️ **Important:** These changes will only apply when upgrading from an installed 8\.8\+ version to the next version




* Starting with SuiteCRM 8\.8\.0 onwards, the upgrade process will now show a warning message with the non\-core files
that will be deleted on upgrade.
* All language files within core directories are now re\-added at the end of the upgrade process.
* Changes to the upgrade process configuration. The following directories were added to the toKeep section:




	+ public/legacy/upload
	+ public/legacy/vendor
	+ .git
	+ .gitignore





#### Platform upgrade



SuiteCRM is now using angular 18\. This brings some system requirement updates and breaking changes.




⚠️ **Important:** SuiteCRM is still using Webpack and Module Federation. We will not be using esbuild and Native Federation for the time being.




**\[For frontend development]** Please check:




* The [Compatibility Matrix](../../compatibility-matrix/) page for the updated versions of the required software.
* The [Frontend extension \- Migrate to SuiteCRM 8\.8\+](../../../../8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/migration/migration-8.8) section for more information on how to upgrade/migrate extensions.





#### System Requirement Changes \- For development



**Minimum node version \- 20\.11\.1**




The minimum node version is now 20\.11\.1\.




**Minimum yarn version \- 4\.5\.0**




The minimum yarn version is now 4\.5\.0\.





#### Backward Incompatible Changes



* **Frontend common lib has been moved into core**




	+ The common lib has been moved from `core/app/common` to `core/app/core/src/lib/common/`
	+ See the [Frontend extension \- Migrate to SuiteCRM 8\.8\+](../../../../8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/migration/migration-8.8) section for more information.
* **The angular.json file is now generated**




	+ The `angular.json` file is now a generated file and is no longer under version control.
	+ See the [Frontend extension \- Migrate to SuiteCRM 8\.8\+](../../../../8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/migration/migration-8.8) section for more information.
* **The configuration for subpanel buttons widgets/stats is now under subpanelWidget**




	+ The configuration key for subpanel button widgets has changed from `insightWidget` to `subpanelWidget`.





#### Documentation



We have added new documentation to set up Two\-factor Authentication using TOTP on SuiteCRM 8 see the [Two\-factor Authentication](../../../../8.x/features/two-factor/two-factor) section for more information.





#### Enhancements



##### PHP 8\.3 Support



This release brings a number of adjustments and updates in order to support PHP 8\.3\.




This includes:




* Removal of deprecated functions/ features.
* Fixed code to eliminate warnings/ errors from logs.
* Update missing labels






#### Filtering on Favorites



You can now filter by `Favorites` and `My Items`.




##### Two\-Factor Authentication



We have added Two\-factor authentication on SuiteCRM 8 using TOTP. See more information [here.](../../../../8.x/features/two-factor/two-factor)




![8.8.0 Enable Two-Factor Authentication](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/Qr-2FA.png)




⚠️ **Important:** **For upgrades from 8\.8\-beta to 8\.8\.0 \- Two Factor TOTP Secret and Backup Codes are now encoded in the db.
This can cause issues when using 2FA configured in the beta. 2FA might need disabled and re\-enabled.**






#### Record View, Subpanel, Top Widget and Timeline Redesign



![8.8.0 Record View](/images/en/8.x/admin/release/new-record-view.png)




* Record View




	+ The fields within the record view now take up minimal space, allowing you to see the full record.
	+ The required field indicator has been moved to the left of the field label.
* Subpanels




	+ Subpanels buttons are now more compact and show the number of records a subpanel has.
	+ The number of subpanels that show when collapsed is configurable via the `subpanel_buttons_panel_limits.yaml`.
	+ Although it is recommended to do these changes within `defaultExt` or alternatively your own extension.
* Timeline




	+ Following the other changes, the timeline is also more compact showing more information on the record without scrolling.
* Top Widget




	+ The Top Widget has been moved to the insights panel, along with some styling adjustments.




##### Demo Data



SuiteCRM Demo Data has been updated to generate more date and optimized to run faster.






#### Log file Change



* The log\_dir has now been updated to log to `<root>/logs/legacy` instead of `<root>/public/legacy`.
* For upgrade instances:




	+ The `log_dir` on config.php will be updated to a new path, only if it was using the default path.
	+ Old log files will not be moved





#### Performance Updates



* Disabled zone js, for faster front\-end performance.
* Added a delay to non\-critical backend calls such as; recently viewed, metadata, and notifications.





#### Record Mappers



Record Mappers have been added to allow for more flexibility when extending SuiteCRM. They allow to add code for mapping a record to internal or to external format.




See the [Record Mappers](../../../../8.x/developer/extensions/backend/record-mappers/) section for more information.





#### Save Handlers



Save handlers are classes that allow the app to run code before or after saving a record. They are similar to the legacy before\_save and after\_save logic hooks.




See the [Save Handlers](../../../../8.x/developer/extensions/backend/save-handlers/) section for more information.





#### Field Logic Update



There are now 3 different types of `triggeringStatus`:




* onDependencyChange




	+ When the dependant field changes.
* onAnyLogic




	+ When anything happens (i.e. value update, rendering fields, saving etc…​).
* onFieldInitialize




	+ When the Field is rendered (i.e. loading the recordview/ switching tabs on recordview).





#### Bug Fixes



* Fixed: Subpanel not showing correctly on first load: [\#511](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/511)
* Fixed: Error field "amount" \- SuiteCRM v. 8\.5\.1: [\#478](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/478)
* Fixed: Reorder modules from User profile does not change the order in navigation bar: [\#585](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/585)
* Fixed: Update scripts deletes files with no warning: [\#475](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/475)
* Fixed: Special character in database password creates issues: [\#533](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/553)
* Fixed: $addr creating unnecessary logs: [\#577](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/577)
* Fixed: Error on Email Detail View: [\#537](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/537)
* Fixed: Accented character (diacritic) displayed incorrectly: [\#542](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/542)
* Fixed: Accented letters displayed incorrectly: [\#583](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/583)
* Fixed: TextArea areas show data in html encoded: [\#589](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/589)
* Fixed: Views Incorrectly Encode UTF Characters as HTML Entities: [\#274](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/274)
* Fixed: Bug with the number field in case filters: [\#572](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/572)
* Fixed: Checkbox taking a while to respond with large data set: [\#597](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/597)
* Fixed: Update New Accounts per Month Chart: [\#75](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/issues/75)

## Seite 82: Front-end Architecture

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/architecture/front-end-architecture`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `6e0b3cca5b195c97b2bf550c8bf05912f6f9efbb623bc9bd179c3cb51110fe4e`

[Issue](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteDocs/issues/new?title=Issue%20about%20Page%208.x/developer/architecture/front-end-architecture.adoc&labels=page-specific&assignee=pgorod "Report an Issue about this Page")

 \> [SuiteCRM 8 Guide](/8.x/) \> [Developer Guide](/8.x/developer/) \> [Architecture](/8.x/developer/architecture/) \> Front\-end Architecture
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 








# Front\-end Architecture






The following documentation is for SuiteCRM Version 8\+; to see documentation for Version 7, click [here](../../../../developer/introduction).







The following documentation assumes that you have some understanding of angular framework






## [1\. Architecture overview](#_1_architecture_overview)




SuiteCRM’s front end architecture was designed with the following concepts in mind.




1. Keep a standard design and approach accross the app




	* Aim to have a very standard UI, with the same look and feel across the app
	* Aim for maximum component re\-usage and develop similar features using the same components
2. Configuration driven




	* Aim to be backend configuration driven as much as possible.
	
	
	
	
		+ Allows for easier customizations
		+ Front\-end extensions are complex and hard to develop
	* No module specific features.
	
	
	
	
		+ If a module has a specific feature it should be developed in a configurable way. Allowing it to be re\-used
3. Extensible




	* Aim to allow extending / overriding all components and services






## [2\. Front\-end structure](#_2_front_end_structure)




SuiteCRM front end components are split into some categories according to their goal.




A good way to start is to have a look at the `app-core` structure in `core/app/core`.
It can provide a high level understanding of how the project is structured and of the existing concepts.




![app_core_folder_structure.png](/images/en/8.x/developer/extensions/front-end/fe-architecture-intro/app_core_folder_structure.png)




While `directives`, `pipes`, `services` and `components` are well known angular concepts. Some of the other may not be that obvious to understand at a first glance.
In the next sub\-chapters we are going to give a high level intro to the concepts behind some of these parts.






## [2\.1 Views](#_2_1_views)




Views is a well known concept in SuiteCRM since way back. Each view represents a page.
In the new front\-end architecture they structure and control all subcomponents in given page.




The following images can help provide a better idea of what a view is on the page.




![listview-view-highlight.png](/images/en/8.x/developer/extensions/front-end/fe-architecture-intro/listview-view-highlight.png)
![recordview-view-highlight.png](/images/en/8.x/developer/extensions/front-end/fe-architecture-intro/recordview-view-highlight.png)




Views could be defined as:




1. The Main component for a page
2. Always linked to a route in the router




	* Thus should always be accessible through a route
3. Can load and control data for a record




	* Done using the concept of stores
4. Control display state for other components in the view
5. Can not be re\-used within other views or components






## [2\.2 Components](#_2_2_components)




Components are the well know components in angular.
However in SuiteCRM there are some extra distinctions




Components could be defined as:




1. Represent a ui re\-usable component




	* they must be re\-usable within any other component
2. Display only




	* Data\-wise, they are considered dumb.
	* Components only display data they don’t change it control it
	* If a component triggers an action or does something that data changes, they do not contain any of the business logic to handle that. They just notify the parent view / container and that action should be handled by the parent
3. Components can call/use other components




Good examples of components are:




* buttons
* panels
* image component
* label component






## [2\.3 Fields](#_2_3_fields)




Fields follow the same concept that know to SuiteCRM since way back.
They represent a field in a record.
A field has a type and is displayed differently depending on that type.




Components could be defined as:




1. Represent a field in the record
2. Can be displayed in modes: `edit`, `create`, `detail`, `list`, `massupdate`, `filter`, and other that may come
3. Can change the way they display depending on metadata
4. They can display and update data




The following image can help give a better understanding




![recordview-fields-highlight.png](/images/en/8.x/developer/extensions/front-end/fe-architecture-intro/recordview-fields-highlight.png)






## [2\.4 Containers](#_2_4_containers)




Containers are very similar to views. Each can load and manage data and each also have and control subcomponents.
Though there is a big difference is: Containers are re\-usable. Containers can be called within other views and aren’t linked to a route.




Containers could be defined as:




1. Representing a set of data: a record, a list, etc.




	* Can load and control data for a record
2. Their data fetching is not tied to the view’s data fetching.
3. They are never linked to a route in the router
4. Control display state for their subcomponents components
5. Can have several instances in a single view




Good examples of containers are:




* Subpanels
* Record selection modals
* Sidebar widgets
* Top widgets




The image we’ve seen previously can help give a better understanding




![recordview-fields-highlight.png](/images/en/8.x/developer/extensions/front-end/fe-architecture-intro/recordview-fields-highlight.png)
















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

# Getting started






The following documentation is for SuiteCRM Version 8\+; to see documentation for Version 7, click [here](../../../developer/introduction).






## [1\. Intro](#_1_intro)




Before starting to develop your extensions you first need to setup a development environment. Since version 8\.3\.0 there are two ways to do this setup.




We recommend using the dev package that is available since version 8\.3\.0\.




Alternatively you can setup an environment directly from the source code, without using any package.




### [1\.1 (recommended) Setting up using the development package](#_1_1_recommended_setting_up_using_the_development_package)



1. Download the dev package from github, e.g.: `SuiteCRM-8.3.0-dev.zip`, see the dev package in the [Latest GitHub Release](https://github.com/salesagility/SuiteCRM-Core/releases/latest)
2. Install SuiteCRM using the [Downloading \& Installing](../../admin/installation-guide/downloading-installing/) guide from the admin section
3. Setup the frontend extension using the [Getting started with frontend extension development](./extensions/frontend/older/8x-fe-extensions-getting-started)





### [1\.2 (alternative) Setting up using the source code](#_1_2_alternative_setting_up_using_the_source_code)



Install from source using the [Backend\-end Developer Install Guide](../installation-guide/backend-end-installation-guide)







## [2\. Understand how extensions work](#_2_understand_how_extensions_work)




The [Extension Structure](../extensions/extension-structure) provide information about the extension structure and guidelines on how to build them.






## [3\. Understand how the application is structured](#_3_understand_how_the_application_is_structured)




The pages in the [Architecture](../architecture/) section provide information on how the application is structured and how it works.






## [4\. Examples](#_4_examples)




If you would like more documentation on building specific extensions. Please see the guides in [Front\-end extension examples](../extensions/frontend/examples/)
















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

## Seite 88: Adding field vardef config mapper

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/backend/fields/vardef-config-mapper`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `2c5e9d472f5aea77a4f282790788465486523d7ffebb797a072a6a9b694370fb`

```
<?php

namespace App\Extension\defaultExt\backend\Module\Service\Fields\Vardefs\FieldDefinitions;

use App\FieldDefinitions\Service\VardefConfigMapperInterface;

class VardefMapper implements VardefConfigMapperInterface
{
    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function getKey(): string
    {
        return 'disable-label-on-vardefs';
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function getModule(): string
    {
        return 'default';
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function map(array $vardefs): array
    {

        foreach ($vardefs as $index => $vardef) {
            $metadata = $vardef['metadata'] ?? [];

            if ($vardef['type'] === 'varchar') {
                $metadata['labelDisplay'] = 'none';
                $vardefs[$index]['metadata'] = $metadata;
            }

        }
        return $vardefs;
    }
}
```

## Seite 90: Introduction to the Process Api

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/backend/process-api/process-api`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `365bff5c0d8469c76569909206d8c2696c3dbf13b5e502db222ee1199e6c39ac`

```
services:
  # default configuration for services in *this* file
  _defaults:
    autowire: true      # Automatically injects dependencies in your services.
    autoconfigure: true # Automatically registers your services as commands, event subscribers, etc.
    public: false       # Allows optimizing the container by removing unused services.
    bind:

       ...

  _instanceof:
    App\Process\Service\ProcessHandlerInterface:
      tags: [ 'app.process.handler' ]
```

## Seite 91: Adding a Process Handler

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/backend/process-api/process-handler`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `dcf9ca2ac969a6c475bcccffe94f8bec0b9c6bd38615a637fab1ac645faae122`

```
<?php

namespace App\Extension\defaultExt\modules\Cases\Service\Fields;

use ApiPlatform\Core\Exception\InvalidArgumentException;
use App\Process\Entity\Process;
use App\Process\Service\ProcessHandlerInterface;

class CaseCalculatePriority implements ProcessHandlerInterface
{
    protected const MSG_OPTIONS_NOT_FOUND = 'Process options are not defined';
    public const PROCESS_TYPE = 'case-calculate-priority';

    /**
     * CaseCalculatePriority constructor.
     */
    public function __construct()
    {
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function getProcessType(): string
    {
        return self::PROCESS_TYPE;
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function requiredAuthRole(): string
    {
        return 'ROLE_USER';
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function getRequiredACLs(Process $process): array
    {
        $options = $process->getOptions();
        $module = $options['module'] ?? '';
        $id = $options['id'] ?? '';

        $editACLCheck =  [
            'action' => 'edit',
        ];

        if ($id !== '') {
            $editACLCheck['record'] = $id;
        }

        return [
            $module => [
                $editACLCheck
            ],
        ];
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function configure(Process $process): void
    {
        //This process is synchronous
        //We aren't going to store a record on db
        //thus we will use process type as the id
        $process->setId(self::PROCESS_TYPE);
        $process->setAsync(false);
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function validate(Process $process): void
    {

        $options = $process->getOptions();
        $type = $options['record']['attributes']['type'] ?? '';
        if (empty($type)) {
            throw new InvalidArgumentException(self::MSG_OPTIONS_NOT_FOUND);
        }
    }

    /**
     * @inheritDoc
     */
    public function run(Process $process)
    {
        $options = $process->getOptions();

        $type = $options['record']['attributes']['type'] ?? '';

        if ($type !== 'User') {
            $priority = $options['record']['attributes']['priority'] ?? '';

            $responseData = [
                'value' => $priority
            ];

            $process->setStatus('success');
            $process->setMessages([]);
            $process->setData($responseData);

            return;
        }

        $name = $options['record']['attributes']['name'] ?? '';

        $value = 'P3';
        if (strpos(strtolower($name), 'warning') !== false) {
            $value = 'P2';
        }

        if (strpos(strtolower($name), 'error') !== false) {
            $value = 'P1';
        }

        $responseData = [
            'value' => $value
        ];

        $process->setStatus('success');
        $process->setMessages([]);
        $process->setData($responseData);
    }
}
```

## Seite 93: Save Handlers

- **Depth:** 2

[Issue](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteDocs/issues/new?title=Issue%20about%20Page%208.x/developer/extensions/backend/save-handlers/_index.en.adoc&labels=page-specific&assignee=pgorod "Report an Issue about this Page") 


[Edit this page](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteDocs/edit/master/content/8.x/developer/extensions/backend/save-handlers/_index.en.adoc "Edit this page")







 \> [SuiteCRM 8 Guide](/8.x/) \> [Developer Guide](/8.x/developer/) \> [Extensions](/8.x/developer/extensions/) \> [Backend](/8.x/developer/extensions/backend/) \> Save Handlers
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 








# Save Handlers





## Intro




Save handlers are classes that allow to run code before or after saving a record. They similar to the legacy before\_save and after\_save logic hooks.




Mappers have been added to allow for more flexibility when extending SuiteCRM.




They are split into type and modes, there are:




Types:
\- Field
\- Field Type
\- Record




Modes:
 \- before\-save
 \- after\-save
 \- save






## Mapper Types




There are 3 mapper types for Save handlers:




### Field Mapper



Field Save Handlers are only executed for the specified module and field. This is useful when you want to modify the data of a specific field (e.g. `phone_mobile` on Accounts).





### Field Type Mapper



Field Save Handlers are executed for all the fields of a given type and for the specified module. This is useful when you want to add a generic transformation to all fields of a given type (e.g. `datetime` on Accounts).





### Record Save Handler



Record Save Handlers is executed at the "record scope", it is possible to change any number of fields for module at the same time. This is useful when you want to modify multiple fields at the same time.







## Save Handlers Modes




Save handlers modes allow you to specify when the mapper should run. One or more of the following modes can be set: before\-save, after\-save.




### Before\-save



This mode is called on the SuiteCRM 8 side before saving a record.





### After\-save



This mode is called on the SuiteCRM 8 side after saving a record.







## Other configurations




### Save Handler Order



When adding a new Save Handler you will have the `getOrder` function to set the order/ priority in which the mappers run per mapper type.

















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `54a6a8d1c1a28b505bcc6adf6a2532a17222fc435502bbb34762220620713061`

```
    'name' => 'website',
    'type' => 'link',
    'label' => 'LBL_WEBSITE',
    'displayParams' => [
        'link_target' => '_blank',
    ],
    'fieldActions' => [
        'klass' => '',
        'containerKlass' => 'd-flex align-items-center',
        'position' => 'inline',
        'actions' => [
            'clear-field' => [
                'key' => 'clear',
                'labelKey' => 'LBL_CLEAR_BUTTON_LABEL',
                'modes' => ['edit'],
                'klass' => ['btn btn-sm btn-secondary p-1 m-0 ml-1'],
                'params' => [
                    'expanded' => true
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ],
```

```
...

$viewdefs['Opportunities'] = [
    'ListView' =>  [
        'sidebarWidgets' => [
            'opportunities-by-sales-stage-price' => [
                'type' => 'chart',
                'labelKey' => 'LBL_QUICK_CHARTS',
                'options' => [
                    'toggle' => true,
                    'headerTitle' => false,
                    'charts' => [
                        [
                            'chartKey' => 'opportunities-by-sales-stage-price',
                            'chartType' => 'horizontal-bar',
                            'statisticsType' => 'opportunities-by-sales-stage-price',
                            'labelKey' => 'PIPELINE_BY_SALES_STAGE',
                            'chartOptions' => [
                            ]
                        ]
                    ]
                ],
                'acls' => [
                    'Opportunities' => ['view', 'list']
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];
        ...
```

## Seite 107: Adding Action Display Logic

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/logic/action-logic/fe-extensions-display-action-logic`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `a92b84dacf4048b25fbdde40507f49c11f62449924350d918b450c27dedb69da`

```
        "recordActions" => [
            "actions" => [
                "print-as-pdf" => [
                    "key" => "print-as-pdf",
                    "labelKey" => "LBL_PRINT_AS_PDF",
                    "asyncProcess" => true,
                    "modes" => ["detail"],
                    "acl" => ["view"],
                    "aclModule" => "AOS_PDF_Templates",
                    "params" => [
                        "selectModal" => ["module" => "AOS_PDF_Templates"],
                    ],
                ],
                "send_fax" => [
                    "key" => "send_fax",
                    "display" => "hide",
                    "asyncProcess" => true,
                    "labelKey" => "LBL_SEND_FAX",
                    "modes" => ["detail"],
                    "acl" => ["view"],
                    "displayLogic" => [
                        "send_fax_visibility" => [
                            "modes" => [
                                0 => "detail",
                                1 => "edit",
                                2 => "create",
                            ],
                            "params" => [
                                "activeOnFields" => [
                                    "phone_fax" => ["01234 999999"],
                                ],
                            ],
                        ],

                    ],
                ],
            ],
        ],
```

## Seite 109: Adding Field Display Logic

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/logic/field-logic/fe-extensions-display-logic`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `4089109a1068d85ac8d5baf223ccfe521705d581d5dfff81f2070b609177a322`

```
<?php

$dictionary['Account']['fields']['phone_office'] = array(
    'name' => 'phone_office',
    'vname' => 'LBL_PHONE_OFFICE',
    'type' => 'phone',
    'dbType' => 'varchar',
    'len' => 100,
    'audited' => true,
    'unified_search' => true,
    'full_text_search' => array('boost' => 1),
    'comment' => 'The office phone number',
    'merge_filter' => 'enabled',
    'displayLogic' => [
        'hide_on_name' => [
            'key' => 'displayType',
            'modes' => [
                'detail',
                'edit',
                'create',
            ],
            'params' => [
                'fieldDependencies' => [
                    'name',
                ],
                'targetDisplayType' => 'none',
                'activeOnFields' => [
                    'name' => ['Example'],
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
);
```

---

```
    array(
      0 =>
      array(
        'name' => 'case_number',
        'label' => 'LBL_CASE_NUMBER',
      ),
      1 => 'priority',
    ),
```

## Seite 111: Adding Required Field Logic

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/logic/field-logic/fe-extensions-required-logic`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `86dbf30968fea21bb4ce1688280a30e8b647449bfb533c039f7747f80fa7bc9f`

```
<?php

$dictionary['Account']['fields']['annual_revenue'] = array(
    'name' => 'annual_revenue',
    'vname' => 'LBL_ANNUAL_REVENUE',
    'type' => 'varchar',
    'len' => 100,
    'comment' => 'Annual revenue for this company',
    'merge_filter' => 'enabled',
    'logic' => [
        'required' => [
            'key' => 'required',
            'modes' => ['edit', 'create'],
            'params' => [
                'fieldDependencies' => [
                    'employees'
                ],
                'activeOnFields' => [
                    'employees' => [
                        '10-50'
                    ],
                ],
            ],
        ]
    ],
);
```

---

## Seite 112: Update field value

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/logic/field-logic/fe-extensions-update-value`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `db102eea643773195baf8d6cf1094ba3e6c53f43f7f097a916679c386597f328`

```
<?php

$dictionary['Account']['fields']['employees'] = array(
    'name' => 'employees',
    'vname' => 'LBL_EMPLOYEES',
    'type' => 'varchar',
    'len' => 10,
    'comment' => 'Number of employees, varchar to accomodate for both number (100) or range (50-100)',
    'logic' => [
        'update-value' => [
            'key' => 'updateValue',
            'modes' => ['detail', 'edit', 'create'],
            'params' => [
                'fieldDependencies' => [
                    'industry',
                ],
                'targetValue' => '450',
                'activeOnFields' => [
                    'industry' => ['Banking'],
                ]
            ]
        ],
    ]
);
```

## Seite 114: Add process driven module menu links

- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `15f09491ddc675f394ecb101203f65f407d3ac8e02297e733952224bc8bff603`

```
<?php

global $mod_strings, $app_strings;

if (ACLController::checkAccess('Cases', 'edit', true)) {
    $module_menu[] = [
        'index.php?module=Cases&action=EditView',
        $mod_strings['LBL_CREATE_CRITICAL_ERROR_CASE'],
        "Create",
        "Cases",
        null,
        '',
        ['process' => 'cases-create-critical-error']
    ];
}
```

## Seite 115: Logic Operators

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/logic/operators`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `18dfcab14bee9cabe6c3f541da15b8f350b786184001c7f61b2f0338dda2fd67`

```
    'name' => 'annual_revenue',
    'comment' => 'Annual revenue for this company',
    'label' => 'LBL_ANNUAL_REVENUE',
    'logic' => [
        'update-when-more-than-10-employees' => [
            'key' => 'updateValue',
            'modes' => ['detail', 'edit', 'create'],
            'params' => [
                'fieldDependencies' => [
                    'employees',
                ],
                'targetValue' => '1000',
                'activeOnFields' => [
                    'employees' =>     [
                        'operator' => 'greater-than',
                        'value' => 10
                    ],
                ]
            ]
        ],
    ]
```

```
        [
            'newTab' => true,

                    'targetDisplayType' => 'hide',
```

```
/* To learn more about this file see: https://angular.io/config/tsconfig. */
{
  "extends": "../../../tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "../../out-tsc/app",

  },
```

 \> [SuiteCRM 8 Guide](/8.x/) \> [Developer Guide](/8.x/developer/) \> [Extensions](/8.x/developer/extensions/) \> [Front\-end](/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/) \> [Migration](/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/migration/) \> Frontend extension \- Migrate to SuiteCRM 8\.8\+
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 








# Frontend extension \- Migrate to SuiteCRM 8\.8\+






The following documentation is for SuiteCRM Version 8\+; to see documentation for Version 7, click [here](../../../../../../developer/introduction).






## [1\. Intro](#_1_intro)




The following guide explains how to migrate extensions created in a version prior to SuiteCRM 8\.8\. So they can work on SuiteCRM 8\.8\+.




In SuiteCRM 8\.8 angular version has been updated to v18\. This brought some changes to the SuiteCRM setup.




⚠️ **Important**: SuiteCRM is still using Webpack and Module Federation. We will not be using esbuild and Native Federation for the time being.






## [2\. Structure Changes](#_2_structure_changes)




**Frontend common lib has been moved into core**




* `core/app/common` is now inside `core/app/core/src/lib/common/`.




**The angular.json file is now generated**




* `angular.json` is now a generated file and is no longer under version control.
* The angular.json file is not part of the base package, it should not be committed.
* Using the `yarn merge-angular-json` it is generated from `base-angular.json` and any extensions' `angular.json`.
* The `base-angular.json` is now under `core/angular/base-angular.json`.
* Each extension should have its own `angular.json` file.




	+ You can use the `angular.json` on defaultExt as a reference.




**Zone.js has been disabled**




* `zone.js` has been disabled (using provideExperimentalZonelessChangeDetection)




**Extension’s webpack.config.js updated**




* ⚠️ **Important**: If using extensions please ensure to update extension configuration.
* Update `webpack.config.js`. (You can use the `webpack.config.js` on `defaultExt` as reference).






## [3\. Command Changes](#_3_command_changes)




**Old yarn build:common removed**




* `yarn run build:common` is no longer needed.




**New yarn build added**




* `yarn build` can be run to build in production.




	+ `yarn build-dev` can be run to build in development.




**New yarn merge\-angular\-json added**




* New command `yarn merge-angular-json` added to merge all angular jsons from any extensions and core and merge them into the generated `angular.json`.




**New build:extension added**




* `yarn build:extension <extensions name>` to build an extension. e.g `yarn build:extension defaultExt`.




	+ Replaces `yarn build:<extension>`, e.g. `yarn build:defaultExt`.
	+ `yarn build-dev:extension <extensions name>` to build in development






## [4\. Steps to migrate the frontend extensions, including defaultExt](#_4_steps_to_migrate_the_frontend_extensions_including_defaultext)




1. Update `node` and `yarn` to the versions specified on the compatibility matrix.




	* You can check the compatibility matrix on the [Compatibility Matrix](../../../../../admin/compatibility-matrix/) page.
2. Unzip the package on the SuiteCRM 8\.8\+ version to somewhere outside the SuiteCRM directory.
3. Update the config files on defaultExt. Copy the following files from the package to your instance.




	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/angular.json`
	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/karma.conf.js`
	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/tsconfig.app.json`
	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/tsconfig.spec.json`
	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/tslint.json`
	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/webpack.config.js`
	* `extensions/defaultExt/app/webpack.prod.config.js`
4. Update the config files on your extension. Using defaultExt as a reference, update the following files.




	* `extensions/<extension>/app/angular.json`
	* `extensions/<extension>/app/karma.conf.js`
	* `extensions/<extension>/app/tsconfig.app.json`
	* `extensions/<extension>/app/tsconfig.spec.json`
	* `extensions/<extension>/app/tslint.json`
	* `extensions/<extension>/app/webpack.config.js`
	* `extensions/<extension>/app/webpack.prod.config.js`
5. Update any references to the common lib used in extensions.




	* defaultExt: `extensions/defaultExt/app/src`.
	* Your extension: `extensions/<extension>/app/src`
	* You can search on the imports for `from 'common'` and replace with `from 'core'`.
6. Update `extension.module.ts` on extensions




	* defaultExt: `extensions/defaultExt/app/src/extension.module.ts`.
	* Your extension: `extensions/<extension>/app/src/extension.module.ts`
	* Use `extensions/defaultExt/app/src/extension.module.ts` from the new package as a reference.
7. Update `remoteEntry` inside `config/extension.php` and your extensions.




	* defaultExt: `extensions/defaultExt/config/extension.php`.
	* Your extension: `extensions/<extension>/config/extension.php`
	* The value of `remoteEntry` has changed from `./extensions/<extension>/remoteEntry.js` to `../extensions/<extension>/remoteEntry.js`
8. Update your custom frontend code on `defaultExt` and your extensions to cope with any breaking changes.
9. Run `yarn merge-angular-json` to generate the `angular.json` file.




	* This will generate the root `angular.json` file based on the `base-angular.json` and any extensions' `angular.json`.
	* The `angular.json` file is not part of the base package, it should not be committed.
	* This generated `angular.json` is used by angular cli to build the main code and the extensions.
10. Build extensions using `yarn build:extension <extension>`.




	* Build defaultExt: `yarn build:extension defaultExt`.
	* Build your extensions: `yarn build:extension <extension>`.
11. ⚠️ You should not need to re\-build the core frontend, as it should be updated during the upgrade.




	* The only reason you would need to build core is if you have made changes to the core. Which we highly discourage.
	* But if you really need, you can run `yarn build` to build the core. This should be done before building the extensions.
















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

## Seite 123: Setting Up a Front-End Extension Module

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/extensions/frontend/older/8x-fe-extensions-setup`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `c1c99c92f96e9e3d195d183225ea8de8afb3ae2364e7330e91bfda16354d8288`

```
const ModuleFederationPlugin = require('webpack/lib/container/ModuleFederationPlugin');

module.exports = {
  output: {
    publicPath: 'auto',
    uniqueName: 'myExt'
  },
  optimization: {
    runtimeChunk: false
  },
  experiments: {
    outputModule: true
  },
  plugins: [
    new ModuleFederationPlugin({

      name: 'myExt',
      filename: 'remoteEntry.js',
      library: {
        type: "module",
      },
      exposes: {
        './Module': './extensions/my-ext/app/src/extension/extension.module.ts'
      },

      shared: {
        '@angular/core': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/common': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/common/http': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/router': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/animations': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/cdk': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/cdk/table': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/cdk/observers': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@angular/forms': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.1.1'
        },
        '@apollo/client': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^3.7.14'
        },
        'angular-svg-icon': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.0.0'
        },
        'apollo-angular': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^5.0.0'
        },
        graphql: {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^16.7.1'
        },
        'lodash-es': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^4.17.20'
        },
        luxon: {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '3.3.0'
        },
        'ng-animate': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^2.0.1'
        },
        'ngx-chips': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^3.0.0'
        },

        '@swimlane/ngx-charts': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^20.3.0'
        },

        '@ng-bootstrap/ng-bootstrap': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^15.0.1'
        },

        'bn-ng-idle': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^2.0.5'
        },

        'rxjs': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^7.8.1'
        },

        'rxjs/operators': {
          singleton: true,
          requiredVersion: '^7.8.1'
        },

        common: {
          singleton: true,
          import: 'dist/common',
          requiredVersion: false
        },

        core: {
          singleton: true,
          import: 'dist/core',
          requiredVersion: false
        },
      }

    }),
  ],
};
```

## Seite 127: Backend-end Developer Install Guide

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/installation-guide/backend-end-installation-guide`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `2e163fbcf778483a772119e8259012932d07781ce047ba90d51a932b1ae62021`

```
find . -type d -not -perm 2755 -exec chmod 2755 {} \;
find . -type f -not -perm 0644 -exec chmod 0644 {} \;
find . ! -user www-data -exec chown www-data:www-data {} \;
chmod +x bin/console
```

## Seite 131: Async Validators option

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/developer/metadata/fields/field-options/async-validator`
- **Depth:** 2
- **Content SHA256:** `76732603bcd30462fc7e4c4e5a3aecc4b514d14229fb067049b8afebfe0ab651`

```
<?php

$dictionary['Account']['fields']['phone_office'] = array(
    'name' => 'phone_office',
    'vname' => 'LBL_PHONE_OFFICE',
    'type' => 'phone',
    'dbType' => 'varchar',
    'len' => 100,
    'audited' => true,
    'unified_search' => true,
    'full_text_search' => array('boost' => 1),
    'comment' => 'The office phone number',
    'merge_filter' => 'enabled',
    'asyncValidators' => [
        'my-async-validator' => [
            'key' => 'my-async-validator',
        ],
    ],
);
```

```
        'static' => [
            'field' => ['accepted-value-1', 'accepted-value-2'],
            'another_field' => 'single-accepted-value'
        ],
        'preset' => [
            'type' => 'preset-filter-key',
            'params' => [
                // module is automatically set to the current module
                // 'module' => '',
                'preset-filter-param-1' => 'param-value',
            ]
        ],
        'attributes' => [
            'filter-attribute' => 'parent-attribute',
            'another-filter-attribute' => 'another-parent-attribute'
        ]
    ]
```

[Issue](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteDocs/issues/new?title=Issue%20about%20Page%208.x/features/two-factor/two-factor.en.adoc&labels=page-specific&assignee=pgorod "Report an Issue about this Page")

 \> [SuiteCRM 8 Guide](/8.x/) \> [SuiteCRM 8 Features](/8.x/features/) \> Two\-Factor Authentication
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 








# Two\-Factor Authentication





## [How to set up Two Factor in SuiteCRM 8?](#_how_to_set_up_two_factor_in_suitecrm_8)




Visit your user profile and there will be a new "Two\-Factor Authentication" tab.




![2FA in Profile](/images/en/8.x/user/features/2FA-Profile.png)




This tab will show the `Status` of the Users 2FA and the configuration button (only `Status` for admins viewing profiles).




The `Two Factor Configuration` button will bring you to the page to enable Two\-Factor Authentication.




![Enable 2FA Page](/images/en/8.x/user/features/Enable-2FA.png)




![2FA QR Code](/images/en/8.x/user/features/Qr-2FA.png)




After enabling 2FA a QR Code will show. Scan that on your authenticator app. Enter the code the app shows.




### [Unable to scan the QR Code?](#_unable_to_scan_the_qr_code)



If you are unable to scan the QR code then you can get the secret to manually input it.




Select `Unable to scan the QR Code?`




![QR Code Secret](/images/en/8.x/user/features/QR-Code-Secret.png)




If you need to add more than the code when setting up manually check out the
[Extra Config](./#_extra_configuration) section.







## [After Enabling](#_after_enabling)




![Enabled 2FA](/images/en/8.x/user/features/Enabled-2FA.png)




After enabling a set of Recovery/ Backup codes will show.




### [What are Recovery/ Backup Codes?](#_what_are_recovery_backup_codes)



Backup codes are one time codes that can be used when you are unable to log in with your authenticator app.
After using a code once you will **not** be able to use it again.




The 10 Backup Codes will only show **after** verifying your authentication code and will **not** show again when revisiting the page.





### [Regenerating Backup Codes](#_regenerating_backup_codes)



You can regenerate backup codes via the Two\-Factor Configuration page.
You will have to re\-enter your authenticator code.




![Regenerate Codes](/images/en/8.x/user/features/Regenerate-Codes.gif)







## [How to Disable Two\-Factor Authentication](#_how_to_disable_two_factor_authentication)




Disabling Two\-Factor Authentication will require you to re\-enter your authentication code.




It can be done via the Two\-Factor Configuration page.




![Disable 2FA](/images/en/8.x/user/features/Disable-Two-Factor.gif)




### [Other ways to disable](#_other_ways_to_disable)



Admins can also disable the 2FA for users via their profile. If the user has 2FA enabled a new "Disable 2FA" action will appear for Admin users, that will allow Admins to disable 2FA for that user.




![Disable via Profile](/images/en/8.x/user/features/New-Disable-2FA.png)







## [Two\-Factor on Login](#_two_factor_on_login)




If 2FA is enabled for your user, after login there will be a popup to enter your authentication code.




![2FA Login](/images/en/8.x/user/features/Login-2FA.png)






## [Extra Configuration](#_extra_configuration)




When setting up Two\-Factor manually on some applications you may be asked to provide more information:




* TOTP
* Time based
* Interval: 30 secs
* Digits: 6
* Sha: 1
















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

[Issue](https://github.com/SuiteCRM/SuiteDocs/issues/new?title=Issue%20about%20Page%208.x/user/daily-activities/managing-drafts.en.adoc&labels=page-specific&assignee=pgorod "Report an Issue about this Page")

 \> [SuiteCRM 8 Guide](/8.x/) \> [User Guide](/8.x/user/) \> [Daily Activities](/8.x/user/daily-activities/) \> Managing Drafts
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 








# Managing Drafts





## [Overview](#_overview)




SuiteCRM 8 automatically saves your email as a draft when closing, ensuring you never lose your work.




Drafts are stored until manually deleted or sent.




Drafts are saved when you close the compose modal if any of the following fields contain content:




* Email body (excluding email signature)
* Subject
* Attachments






## [Managing Drafts](#_managing_drafts)




### [Draft Saved Notification](#_draft_saved_notification)



When you close the compose modal and a draft is saved, the "Draft has been saved." label briefly appears on screen to confirm your email has been preserved.





### [Drafts Button](#_drafts_button)



When drafts are saved, a button appears in the bottom right corner of the screen displaying:




* The current number of drafts
* `10+` if there are more than 10 drafts




![Drafts Button](/images/en/8.x/user/daily-activities/drafts-button.png)




Click this button to view your list of saved drafts.





### [Draft List](#_draft_list)



![Draft List](/images/en/8.x/user/daily-activities/draft-list.png)




The draft list displays all your saved drafts with the following information for each:




* **Recipient**: Shows the first "To" record, or the Related To record if it’s in the To list, or "No Recipients" if none specified
* **Subject**: The beginning of the email subject
* **Last Modified**: Date the draft was last modified





### [Editing a Draft](#_editing_a_draft)



To edit a draft, click on the item in the draft list.




When editing a draft:




* The modal header displays "Draft" instead of "New Email"
* A trash icon appears in the bottom left corner to dismiss the current draft
* The draft will not appear in the draft list while being edited




![Editing a Draft](/images/en/8.x/user/daily-activities/draft-edit.png)





### [Deleting a Draft](#_deleting_a_draft)



There are currently 2 ways to delete a draft:




* The trash icon that shows on the draft list
* The trash icon in the compose modal when editing a draft





### [Dismiss All Drafts](#_dismiss_all_drafts)



Click the "Dismiss All" button at the top of the draft list to delete all drafts at once. An inline confirmation (Cancel/Confirm) will appear before deletion.




ℹ️ Drafts currently being edited will not be dismissed when using "Dismiss All".





### [Disable Drafts Popup](#_disable_drafts_popup)



To disable the drafts popup, navigate to Admin \> Email Settings and toggle off "Enable Drafts Popup".




When disabled, drafts will still be saved but the popup will not appear when closing the compose modal.

















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

 \> [SuiteCRM 8 Guide](/8.x/) \> [User Guide](/8.x/user/) \> [Daily Activities](/8.x/user/daily-activities/) \> Notes \& Attachments
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 








# Notes \& Attachments





## [Overview](#_overview)




The Notes module in SuiteCRM can be used to keep a record of any
comments, observations or explanations that a User may have relating
internally to their organisation or relating to another SuiteCRM record
such as an Account, Contact, Lead or many more. Notes are also used to
keep record of interactions with Customers regarding Cases and Bugs.






## [Note Actions](#_note_actions)




Access Note actions from the module menu:




* **Create Note or Attachment** – Opens a form to create a new Note record.
* **View Notes** – Redirects to the List View for searching and listing Note records.






## [Creating a Note](#_creating_a_note)




Navigate to the Notes module and select "Create Note" from the Actions menu.




![Note Create View](/images/en/8.x/user/daily-activities/notes-edit.png)




### [Fields Overview](#_fields_overview)



* **Subject** \- Subject of the note
* **Assigned To** \- User responsible for the document
* **Related To** \- The Parent record the note is related to (Account, Contact, Lead, etc.)
* **Contact** \- The Contact associated with the note (if applicable). This field will dynamically hide if the Related To is Contacts.
* **Note** \- Note Content
* **Attachment** \- The file attached to the note (if applicable)

















Content is available under [GNU Free Documentation License 1\.3](http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/fdl.html) or later unless otherwise noted.

## Seite 152: Campaigns

- **URL:** `https://docs.suitecrm.com/8.x/user/modules/_campaigns`
